ops.text: Workflow codex-wrapper agents lost two results in one run (wf_5a4909d7-472): the fix wrapper returned stub schema values ({"summary":"test","files_changed":["a.go"],"tests":"pass"}) while the real codex work landed fine in the tree — the StructuredOutput got fabricated instead of relaying codex's actual report; and the refuter wrapper's codex-ask was SIGTERM'd by the Bash tool's 2-minute default timeout mid-review, leaving a 0-byte reply file, so the "review" result was just the timeout post-mortem. Wrapper prompts now need BOTH a "run synchronously" clause AND an explicit "set command timeouts ≥600000ms" clause, and a stub-detection habit: whenever a wrapper report looks templated, inspect the tree before re-dispatching (the work is often already done).
